body: Need to ship the new auth flow this week
Goal is to remove stale key reuse across clients
Setup: three clients, one auth provider, shared localStorage
Constraints: cannot break the mobile app session
Auth flow is broken; users see login loop on token refresh
Sessions expire silently; no error surfaced to user
Token cache is missing on the desktop client
Issue: clients each ship their own auth code path
Audit shows three clients writing to different keys
Trace: refresh token never persists past first reload
Root cause is racing localStorage writes between tabs
Auditing the redux store shows duplicate auth slices
Decision: single source of truth in shared storage adapter
We will pick a canonical key name and migrate all clients
Decided: use IndexedDB instead of localStorage
Locked in: refresh tokens stored under auth.refresh
Build the storage adapter with read/write/clear primitives
Implementing the new auth interceptor in the API client
Wrote the migration helper that copies legacy keys
Update the redux middleware to dispatch on token refresh
Added a hook to listen for storage events across tabs
Fix: removed stale key cache; tests green
Patch the race condition by serializing writes
Fixed the silent expiry by emitting an error event
Patched the migration to handle missing legacy keys
Review: LGTM, suggest renaming _authKey to _authToken
Reviewed; the adapter API matches the spec
Spec review passed; one nitpick on naming
LGTM. Approved.
Ship it; deploying to production now
Deployed; demo recorded; closing the loop
Cut a release tag and pushed
Demo-ready; flipped the feature flag for everyone
Next: write the migration runbook for ops
Follow-on: backfill the analytics events
Queueing the i18n pass as a follow-up
Deferred: the dark mode color tokens until v0.3
Goal: get the dashboard pulling live data by Friday
Current state: dashboard is showing stale fixtures
Setup: nine widgets, one fetch hook each
Constraints: cannot exceed 5s initial paint
The dashboard is broken on Safari; nothing renders
Issue: fetch hook crashes on null response
Missing error boundary in the chart container
Problem is the polling interval is too aggressive
Audit: 15 fetch calls fire on initial mount
Trace shows nine widgets each polling separately
Root cause: no shared cache for the metrics endpoint
Audited the network panel; same call repeated 9x
Decision: introduce a shared SWR cache layer
Pick the swr library over react-query for this
Locked: every widget reads through the metrics provider
Decided: throttle to one poll per 30s globally
Implemented the metrics provider with SWR
Build the throttle middleware around the fetcher
Wrote the unit tests for the cache invalidation rules
Generating the chart components from the new schema
Fix: handled the null-response crash with a guard clause
Patch the safari rendering bug by removing flexbox gap
Fixed the duplicate polls by sharing the cache key
Patched the chart container with a fallback boundary
Reviewed: the cache shape matches our spec
Review pass: no regressions, perf is back to 3s paint
LGTM, ship it after staging soak
Spec review confirms throttle behaves correctly
Shipped; rolled out to all production users
Deployed the metrics provider; cut tag v2.4.0
Demo-ready; recorded the walkthrough video
Ship: production traffic is on the new path
Next: add a system status indicator to the header
Follow-on work: localize the metric names
Queue: build a downloadable CSV export
Deferred: real-time WebSocket updates to v3
Goal: fix the broken onboarding email links
State: signups still happening, links 404
Setup: SES emails point at the staging domain
Constraints: cannot cycle the verified sender for 7 days
Problem: prod links land on staging.example.com
Issue: the email template uses the wrong base URL
Missing env var for production rollout
Broken: every onboarding link 404s after merge
Audit: env var was renamed in the last refactor
Trace shows ENV.APP_URL never gets read at template time
Root cause: legacy env name used in production secrets
Audited the deployed manifest; no APP_URL key set
Decision: rename to APP_PUBLIC_URL across all templates
Locked: the secret will be set in the prod manifest tonight
Decided to keep both keys for one release for safety
Pick the new name to match the public-facing convention
Build: updated all six email templates with new var
Implemented the env-loader fallback for the legacy key
Wrote a CI check that fails on missing env vars
Generated the release notes for the env rename
Fix: patched the template render to log when var is unset
Fixed the staging-vs-prod mismatch in the deployer
Patched the broken link by setting the env var
Fix: handled the missing var with a clear error message
Review: sample emails sent and verified in inbox
Reviewed and approved; staging signoff complete
LGTM with a nit: capitalize the URL fragment
Ship it; rolled out to production at 14:00 UTC
Deployed; closed the bug ticket; comms sent
Next: instrument the link-open events for tracking
Follow-on: deprecate the legacy env name in v3.1
